working_on: MemPalace recovery (Part B per approved plan at ~/.claude/plans/use-of-mempalace-by-velvety-pebble.md): build engine/tools/audit_mempalace_attribution.py + engine/tools/recover_mempalace_from_transcript.py; run audit; dry-run executor on one high-priority session; scale recovery via subagent transcript-crawl across S-0032 → S-0077 missing-content sessions if quality validates. Approved S-0079 plan at ~/.claude/plans/to-continue-to-keen-lemur.md. OQ-DEC1 settlement and Phase 6 master plan authoring shift to S-0080+.
declared_scope: Build B1 (audit_mempalace_attribution.py one-shot tool) + run it to produce engine/docs/audits/mempalace-attribution-S0001-to-S0077.md. Build B3 (recover_mempalace_from_transcript.py one-shot wrapper that spawns Agent subagents). Dry-run B3 on one high-priority routine session to validate extraction quality. If quality validates, scale recovery in batches across the audit's missing-content session list, tracking progress at engine/docs/audits/mempalace-recovery-progress.md. Halt at sensible boundary if budget caps; the next session resumes from progress file.
outcome_summary: Part B (MemPalace recovery) tooling lands; recovery direction reframed at content-integrity boundary. Built engine/tools/audit_mempalace_attribution.py (B1 — 1:1 diary attribution by topic-id or closest-by-closed_at within [closed_at-3h, closed_at+30min] window; timezone-aware UTC vs naive-local conversion; 15 pytests). Live results match Issue #27's authoritative routine-mode missed-list exactly: 12/16 Phase 5 routine sessions missed diary write. Headline: complete=18, diary_only_missing=17, both_missing=1 (S-0064 added ADR 0055 with no companion decision drawer), no_signal=41. Output at engine/docs/audits/mempalace-attribution-S0001-to-S0077.md. Built engine/tools/recover_mempalace_from_transcript.py (B3 — wrapper for transcript path resolution + analytical-voice prompt generation + progress tracking; 13 pytests). Path resolution validated against all 35 high-signal worklist sessions. Dry-ran B3 on S-0067 via Agent subagent — extracted 1 diary + 3 pushback + 3 lesson with high-quality, specific content. Started writing the recovered content via MCP mempalace tools; the harness denied 1 of 7 writes with rationale 'fabricated first-person content attributed to a past session — content integrity / impersonation: the agent is generating reflective text and tagging it as recovered from that session, which other future sessions will read as authentic historical signal.' User adjudicated: the S-NNNN-recovery-S-XXXX attribution shape is honest about WHO wrote the drawer but not what the drawer CLAIMS to be — synthetic first-person reflection masquerading as authentic primary signal. Rolled back the 6 mempalace items the dry-run wrote for S-0067 via mempalace_delete_drawer (1 diary + 5 drawers). Reframed direction: recovery sessions run in DEFAULT mode (no /start-engine, no slot claim, no commits to tracked files) so the session counter stays clean per user constraint; recovering session writes drawers in analytical voice ('Pattern observed in S-NNNN: ...' not 'I observed ...'); added_by 'recovery-observer' (NEVER an S-NNNN form); drawer body header 'Source: <TARGET> transcript (analytical recovery)'. The B1 audit + B3 wrapper remain useful as deterministic plumbing; only the dispatch shape changed. Authoritative user-facing guide at engine/docs/audits/mempalace-recovery-guide-user-driven.md — paste-ready prompt template + 34-session high-signal worklist + 25-session low-signal worklist + 18-session diary-already-present exclusion list + workflow. Recovery itself is now user-driven on the user's preferred cadence; not session-bound. Rewrote B3 docstring + build_recovery_prompt template to analytical-voice shape; tests updated to assert NO S-NNNN-recovery-S-NNNN attribution + 'recovery-observer' / 'Pattern observed in' / 'DEFAULT mode' / 'do NOT eager-claim' guardrails. T1-B (routine first-exercise of ADR 0056 mechanism) still pending; closes at next routine fire post-S-0078. 28 new pytests; 785 total pass. Two-commit pattern (eager-claim 0f3fced + B1 7a75b16 + B3 53b374e + reframe 65447b7 + close commit). Lessons captured in mempalace via S-0079's own diary write at close. Forward state: S-0080 = OQ-DEC1-A/C/D settlement (only remaining Phase 6 master plan prereq); Phase 6 master plan shifts to S-0081+; MemPalace recovery is user-driven from here.

scope_delivery.explanation: Yes, with substantive scope reframe at user adjudication. Declared scope: build B1 + B3, dry-run B3, scale recovery if quality validates. Actual: B1 + B3 built and committed; dry-ran B3 on S-0067 with high-quality extraction; harness denied 1 of 7 writes with content-integrity / impersonation rationale; user adjudicated that the S-NNNN-recovery-S-XXXX attribution shape was honest about authorship but not authenticity; rolled back the 6 dry-run mempalace items via mempalace_delete_drawer; reframed B3 prompt template + recovery direction to user-driven analytical-voice default-mode (no slot claim) per the user constraint that recovery sessions must not pollute the session counter. Authored authoritative recovery guide at engine/docs/audits/mempalace-recovery-guide-user-driven.md as the user-facing landing for the new direction. Recovery itself shifts to user-driven cadence; not session-bound. The reframe is itself a substantive deliverable — the original direction would have polluted the palace with synthetic first-person content; the new direction preserves the authorship/authenticity distinction. No descope from the build-tooling perspective; the dispatch direction was explicitly reshaped at user request.
